$comment: Slice 069 — per-package Go line-coverage floors enforced by cmd/scripts/coverage-gate. Slice 279 — measurement extended to MERGED unit + integration profiles via gocovmerge (see docs/coverage-audit-2026-05.md). Slice 312 — round-3 refresh: 13 new-package floors added (auth-substrate-v2 + MCP + export + platform + sdk-go/oauth), 9 ratchet-up lifts, 4 new excludes (CLI scripts + 1-stmt embed + 0-stmt emptyset). See docs/coverage-audit-2026-05-round-3.md for the per-package rationale. Slice 315 — 4 new auth-substrate-v2 small-package floors added (oauthclient 84, oauthcode 85, revocation 77, userprefs 83) with new integration_test.go suites + CI integration-enrollment. Slice 317 — 2 new MCP write-proposals floors added (internal/api/mcpwriteproposals 82, internal/mcp/writeproposals 75) with new unit suites + CI integration-enrollment (existing integration_test.go was never enrolled). Slice 319 — internal/questionnaire floor added at 77 (measured 79.32% merged; 324 statements; questionnaire engine — Excel parser + AnswerLibrary RLS lookup + PDF render helpers + Store CRUD), with a new in-package integration_test.go + pdf_test.go + store_helpers_test.go + CI integration-enrollment. Slice 313 — 5 new admin HTTP handler floors added (adminauditperiods 71, adminsuperadmins 70, admintenants 70, adminvendors 75, tenants 74) via CI integration-enrollment of pre-existing integration_test.go suites + 3 new helpers_test.go suites covering pure-Go pre-DB branches (slice 290 pattern). Slice 318 — 3 new audit-log family floors added (internal/audit umbrella 70, internal/audit/sink 70, internal/audit/unifiedlog 70) via CI integration-enrollment of pre-existing audit + sink integration_test.go suites + new in-package unifiedlog integration_test.go + helpers_test.go (umbrella pure-Go predicate guards + pre-tx validation) + sink helpers_test.go (Default reader + Shutdown ctx-cancel + no-op discard) + unifiedlog joinkinds_test.go (CSV serializer + AllKinds order contract). Slice 320 — internal/demoseed floor added at 81 (measured 83.1% merged; 522 statements; slice 205 demo dataset — Apply orchestrator + Teardown + 17 writer helpers + buildFixtures + per-primitive INSERTs) via CI integration-enrollment of the pre-existing slice-205 integration_test.go (Apply happy / idempotent / refuses-populated / cross-tenant isolation / scale / invalid-slug) + new helpers_test.go (15 pure-Go writer-helper branches: capitalize / kindToConnector / riskScoreJSON / fictionalUserEmail / buildEvidencePayload default / withTenant+currentTenantOf / nullableUUID / nonZeroOrSelf / nonZeroOrTenant / periodStatus / frozenHashOrNil / frozenByOrNil / sha256Of) + new seeder_test.go (NewSeeder nil-pool sentinel / WithClock mutator / validateSlug enumerated bad-input branches / pgIsUndefinedTable sentinel detection / applyScale scale-clamp edges / hashCanonicalJSON determinism / hexString shape + constant invariants for DemoSeedVersion / DefaultScale / PopulatedRowCap). Same playbook as slices 290 / 297 / 310 / 315 — enrolment is the load-bearing move. Append-only invariant honored — every test seeds via INSERT only; cleanup via admin BYPASSRLS role, never the app role. Slice 314 — internal/api/oauth floor added at 72 (measured 74.7% merged; 921 statements; the slice-187+ OAuth 2.0 AS HTTP surface — RFC 9068 JWT profile + RFC 8693 token-exchange + RFC 7636 PKCE + RFC 8628 device grant + RFC 7009 revocation + RFC 7662 introspection). Two-pronged per the slice disposition: (a) CI integration-enrolment of the pre-existing slice-187/188/189/190/191 integration suites (they carried the //go:build integration tag but were never in the tests-integration package list, so had never run in CI); (b) new unit suites covering the pre-DB RFC-conformance branches (token_test/authorize/introspect/revoke/wellknown/device + helpers) PLUS a new user_resolver_integration_test.go exercising the slice-192 DBUserResolver (the authorize suite used a stub resolver, leaving the real readSessionIdentity+queryUserRoles+ResolveForOAuth at 0%). Enrolment surfaced a latent device-flow integration-test bug (postForm helper misuse → 404) that the unenrolled state had hidden; fixed in-suite. Same enrolment-is-the-move playbook as slices 290/297/310/313/315/317/318/319/320. Slice 422 — internal/api/oauth floor lifted 72 → 77 (measured merged 79.0%, up from 74.7% at slice 314). This is a security-critical error-branch coverage lift toward the slice-350 90% advisory: it adds the dangerous RFC DENY branches that the happy-path-weighted slice-314 floor left untested (slice 333 Q-4). New unit suite error_branches_test.go drives the pre-DB DENY branches reachable without Postgres — RFC 8693 token-exchange refusals (missing/unsupported subject_token_type, missing/non-UUID target tenant, expired/wrong-issuer/wrong-audience subject_token → invalid_token, denied tenant-switch → invalid_target with a no-internal-detail-leak assertion composing with slice 367), client_credentials missing-credentials → invalid_request, malformed-form rejections across /oauth/token + /oauth/introspect + /oauth/revoke + /oauth/device_authorization, the revoke Basic-auth-no-fallthrough spoofing guard, and the endpoint-constructor fail-loud panic invariants. New integration tests cover the DB-state-dependent DENY arms: device_deny_integration_test.go (RFC 8628 §3.5 expired_token + cross-client invalid_grant) and two introspect {active:false} arms in revoke_introspect_integration_test.go (expired-token + forged-signature must report inactive, not active and not 401). Every new test asserts the specific RFC error CODE, not merely the HTTP status (AC-7). The 90 advisory in $security_critical_packages is unchanged — this lifts the hard floor only. Slice 426 — targeted coverage-lift round across five low-floor user-facing / eval-adjacent surfaces, following the slice-353 Q-2 pure-Go fast-loop convention (a helpers_test.go alongside each package's existing integration suite, exercising validators / normalizers / wire converters / pre-tx input guards / handler deny branches with fast t.Parallel() table tests — no Postgres, no build tag). Floors lifted (merged unit+integration, floor(measured-2pp)): internal/api/decisions 18 → 37 (measured 39.52%), internal/api/me 43 → 50 (measured 52.14%), internal/api/policies 39 → 49 (measured 51.33%), internal/freshnessdrift 19 → 24 (measured 26.44%), internal/policy 35 → 70 (measured 72.75%). The policy/me/policies tests assert RLS/role/identity DENY branches (missing-context 401, non-approver 403, missing/invalid caller identity), not happy-path-only, with nil stores proving no DB call is reached on the guard path (slice 333 Q-4 + P0-426-3/4). freshnessdrift gained the least because Scheduler.Run / SweepOnce / RefreshSubscriber.Start genuinely require NATS + Postgres and stay integration-only (AC-6 residual, documented in the helpers_test.go header). See docs/audit-log/426-coverage-round-decisions.md. Slice 456 — internal/api/oauth floor lifted 77 → 78 (measured merged 80.8%, up from 79.0% at slice 422). This is the residual security-critical error-arm lift toward the slice-350 90% advisory: it covers the arms slice 422 deliberately left for the tail. NEW unit suite residual_branches_test.go drives the pre-DB arms — the token-exchange signer-failure (verify-ok/sign-fail keystore: a non-ES256 P-384 active signing key makes tokensign.Sign fail the curve check while Verify still resolves the real fsstore verification keys, so a self-minted subject_token verifies then the exchange's own Sign step 500s with server_error and a no-internal-detail-leak assertion composing with slice 367), the tokenBucketLimiter overflow-cap + WindowSeconds/max edge arms (via new unexported export_test.go seams ExportNewTokenBucketLimiter / ExportLimiterAllow / ExportLimiterWindowSeconds), the NewTokenEndpoint rate<=0 fallback, and the writeAudit / writeAuthCodeAudit nil-pool early returns. NEW integration suite residual_audit_integration_test.go drives the DB-state-dependent arms — the best-effort audit-write FAILURE arms (BeginTx failure via a closed pool; Exec failure via an AfterConnect search_path-stripped pool that resolves the unqualified oauth_token_exchanges to no relation; empty-jti 'unknown' fallback verified via an RLS-scoped read; jti-truncation; from_tenant non-nil + NULL branches) each asserting the D3 best-effort non-blocking outcome (no panic, no caller-visible error), and the signer-failure server_error arms for client_credentials (token.go:272), authorization_code (authorize.go:479), and device_code (device_code_grant.go:127) — each reaching the JWT mint after a real Verify/redemption and asserting 500 + server_error. The 90 advisory in $security_critical_packages is unchanged — this lifts the hard floor only. RESIDUAL gap to 90 (AC-4): the writeAudit/writeAuthCodeAudit ApplyTenant-failure arms (token.go:410 / pkce.go:106) are defensive dead code — ApplyTenant's SET LOCAL set_config always succeeds on a healthy tx, so the arm is only reachable on a connection broken mid-transaction; and the device-approval browser flow (device_approve.go ServeApprove/ServeDeny) + the DBUserResolver BYPASSRLS authPool path (user_resolver.go enumerateMemberships/lookupSuperAdmin, slice-192 surface) are out of slice 456's named scope (audit-write + signer-failure + rate-limiter) and are filed as a spillover. See docs/audit-log/456-oauth-coverage-residual-decisions.md. Slice 472 — internal/api/oauth floor lifted 78 → 87 (measured merged 89.3%, up from 80.8% at slice 456). This is the slice-456 spillover: it covers the two surfaces slice 456 named out-of-scope, both in the slice-350 security-critical tier. (1) The slice-191 device-APPROVAL browser flow — NEW integration suite device_approval_integration_test.go mounts the real ServeApprove/ServeDeny handlers on the public Handler (via AttachDeviceApprovalEndpoint, oauth.go:151, 0% before) behind a credential-injecting middleware against a real DeviceCodeStore, asserting the specific RFC error code (not merely the status) for every arm: approve success (snapshot written + verified via LookupByUserCode, device_authorization.go:366, 0% before) + the cred.TenantID!='' snapshot-build branch + the no-tenant snapshot branch; the Approve 0-rows error-mapping arms — not_found→404 invalid_request, expired→400 expired_token, denied/consumed→400 invalid_grant, already-approved race→400 invalid_grant; deny success (consumed_at set, snapshot NOT written) + deny not_found→404. (2) The slice-192 DBUserResolver BYPASSRLS authPool path — NEW integration suite user_resolver_authpool_integration_test.go wires NewDBUserResolverWithAuthPool (0% before) with the BYPASSRLS admin pool (DATABASE_URL) and drives enumerateMemberships (cross-tenant: one OIDC subject across TWO tenants → both in available_tenants, per-tenant roles resolved from the correct tenant-scoped user_id) + the session-fallback append branch (user_resolver.go:261, session user 'disabled' so the active-filter enumeration misses it then the fallback re-adds it) + lookupSuperAdmin BOTH ways (present→super_admin true, absent→false; P0-188-4 super_admin never synthesized). NEW pure-Go unit file device_approval_constructor_test.go covers the NewDeviceApprovalEndpoint nil-codes fail-loud panic + nil-Now default (slice-353 Q-2 fast loop). RESIDUAL gap to 90 (AC-3): the remaining ~0.7pp is genuinely hard-to-reach — the resolver's readSessionIdentity/queryUserRoles BeginTx/ApplyTenant mid-transaction-failure arms (defensive: reachable only on a connection broken mid-tx), readRandom's crypto-failure arm, the Consume race-window arms, and a long tail of other-slice initiate/authorize/introspect/revoke error paths (device_authorization.go ServeHTTP, authorize.go, introspect.go, revoke.go) outside slice 472's named scope (device-approval + DBUserResolver). The 90 advisory in $security_critical_packages is unchanged — this lifts the hard floor only. At 89.3% measured the package is now ~0.7pp under the advisory; a final slice covering the cross-package initiate/authorize/introspect/revoke error tail could promote 90 from advisory to hard floor, but that promotion is deliberately NOT made here (it would require touching the advisory mechanism + draining arms owned by slices 187/189/190/191). See docs/audit-log/472-oauth-coverage-tail-decisions.md.
$methodology: Floors are derived from a measured run on main. Slice 069 used a unit-only profile; slice 279 measures against the gocovmerge-merged unit + integration profile. Each floor is set at `max(0, floor(measured - 2pp))` — the floor RATCHETS the current actual minus a 2-pp noise band, never reaches above it (slice 069 P0-A4).
$how_to_raise: Raise a per-package floor in a follow-up slice that (a) writes the additional tests to actually hit the new bar, (b) lifts the number here in the same PR. Do NOT raise floors in a slice that does not also write the tests. The ratchet contract is monotonic ↑ — never lower a floor.
$how_to_extend: When a new package lands, add a row here in the same PR. Default floor is `floor(merged_measured - 2pp)`. If a package is intentionally untested (sqlc-generated, protoc-generated, integration-tested elsewhere), add it to `excludes` instead.

$security_critical_packages.$comment: Slice 350 — security-critical tier MARKER. A named subset of the auth-substrate-v2 + tenancy spine where an error-branch regression is the worst outcome the platform can produce. Tier membership is the marker; the hard per-package floor stays in `thresholds` (slice 069 contract, unchanged). This block adds an ADVISORY-only check on top: coverage-gate prints a warning (not a failure) when a tier package falls below `advisory_target_pct`. The advisory is intentionally non-blocking — lifting hard floors toward 90% is per-package follow-up slice work (each one writes the tests AND lifts the threshold in the same PR per the slice 069 monotonic-ratchet contract).
$security_critical_packages.$trigger: Slice 333 Q-4 in docs/audits/333-qa-strategy-gap-analysis.md: a 75% line-floor on `internal/api/oauth` can be satisfied by happy-path coverage while dangerous error branches (invalid grant, expired code, revoked token, tenant-switch denied, super_admin escalation refused) sit untested.
$security_critical_packages.$measurement_caveat: Go's `-coverprofile=` (in any -covermode: set / count / atomic) emits per-basic-block profiles. This is closer to statement coverage than to McCabe branch coverage — for simple conditionals each block effectively represents one arm, but for compound predicates (`a && b`, `a || b`) the gate cannot distinguish which sub-predicate caused a branch to be skipped. The tier is therefore a more-aggressive STATEMENT-coverage advisory on a named subset of packages, not literal branch coverage. See docs/audit-log/350-branch-coverage-floor-decisions.md D1 for the toolchain rationale.
$security_critical_packages.$advisory_vs_hard: Two checks per tier package: (1) HARD floor from `thresholds` enforced as a gate failure (existing slice 069 behavior, unchanged). (2) ADVISORY 90% target — coverage-gate emits a warning to stderr when measured coverage < advisory_target_pct, but does NOT fail. The advisory exists for visibility without blocking CI; future slices each lift a package's hard floor toward 90% as they enrol new tests.
$security_critical_packages.$how_to_lift_toward_advisory: Each lift is its own slice. A lift slice (a) writes the missing tests, (b) raises the package's hard floor in `thresholds`. The ratchet contract is monotonic ↑ — never lower a hard floor.
$security_critical_packages.$how_to_extend_roster: Extending beyond auth-substrate-v2 + tenancy requires explicit justification — this tier exists for the load-bearing security primitives. Round-2 expansion may add packages once the value of the tier is demonstrated.
$security_critical_packages.$roster_rationale: internal/auth/{jwt,keystore,tokensign,oauthclient,oauthcode,revocation,userprefs} = JWT signing + OAuth code grant + revocation + user-prefs primitives. internal/api/oauth = the OAuth 2.0 AS HTTP surface (slice 187+). internal/api/authzmw = the authorization middleware enforcing RBAC + super_admin gates. internal/tenancy = the RLS context-propagation primitive.

$tier_recommendations.$comment: Slice 279 — aspirational per-tier targets. NOT enforced. The `thresholds` map continues to enforce per-package floors via cmd/scripts/coverage-gate; this tier block exists as MAINTAINER GUIDANCE for setting floors on NEW packages and for the spillover backlog.

api_handlers_and_business_logic.rationale: API handlers, business logic, and RLS-touching code are the highest-value test surfaces — every regression here is user-facing.

connector_business_logic.rationale: Connector internals (auth + the per-source extractor) are the load-bearing code; slice 069 already set most at 80%+.

cli_cmd_glue.rationale: cmd/atlas, cmd/atlas-cli, and the connectors' cmd/atlas-* binaries are cobra glue + main entry points. They are integration-tested via the self-host bundle smoke job; no unit-test pressure.

generated_code.rationale: sqlc-generated, protoc-generated code. Already in the `excludes` array; tested transitively through the wrapping package.

$exclusions_comment: Packages that the gate skips entirely. These are either auto-generated (sqlc, protoc) or main-package wrappers integration-tested elsewhere (the connectors' cmd/atlas-* binaries are unit-listed above; cmd/atlas + cmd/atlas-cli are integration-tested by the self-host bundle e2e job).
$exclude_justifications_comment: Slice 353 (Q-5 from slice 333's QA audit). Every prefix in `excludes` MUST have a matching key here with a written justification. Enforced by scripts/check-coverage-excludes.sh (self-tested by scripts/check-coverage-excludes_test.sh). This is the structural fix for Theme 2 of the audit (opt-in lists bit-rot): an exclude can no longer be added silently. Quarterly maintainer review: walk each entry, re-justify in place OR file a slice to retire the exclude (write the tests + remove the entry + re-floor in the same PR per the slice 069 monotonic-ratchet contract). The `$`-prefix keeps this block invisible to coverage-gate's JSON struct (unknown fields ignored).

$exclude_justifications.cmd/scripts/coverage-check/: the coverage gate/check tooling itself — bootstrap tooling not subject to its own gate.
$exclude_justifications.cmd/scripts/coverage-gate/: the coverage gate/check tooling itself — bootstrap tooling not subject to its own gate.
